title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
We identified 4 clusters of innate lymphocytes that were CD161(KLRB1)+
CD3(CD3D/CD3G)-, consisting of ILC1/3, ILC2, ILC1/Natural Killer (NK) and NK
(KLRD1+, GNLY+, PRF1+, GZMB+ and FCGR3A+) cells

Plasticity within ILC1
and ILC3 is also recognized, as reflected in our annotation of ILC1/3

Fetal skin ILCs (IL7R+, RORC+ and KIT+)
resemble adult skin ILC3
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) annotate ILC1/3 as a CD161+CD3- innate lymphocyte cluster reflecting ILC1-ILC3 plasticity in adult skin. This corresponds to ILC1 (ILC1_3) in Gopee 2024. Fetal skin ILCs resemble adult ILC3.

title: Natural killer cells in skin: a unique opportunity to better characterize the many facets of an overlooked secondary lymphoid organ
quotes: scRNA-seq of lesional vs non-lesional skin identified four unique clusters of innate lymphocytes in skin that were CD161(KLRB1)+/CD3(CD3D/CD3G)neg. These subgroups included ILC1/3, ILC2, ILC1/NK cells, and NK (KLRD1+, GNLY+, PRF1+, GZMB+, and FCGR3A+) cells

Some propose that ILC2 and ILC3 cells are predominantly tissue resident in healthy homeostatic states, whereas ILC1 cells continuously traffic between circulation and lymph nodes in a CD62L-and CCR7-dependent manner
summary: Johnson and Lee (2025) confirm the four-cluster ILC annotation from Reynolds 2021 in adult human skin. ILC1 cells may continuously traffic via CCR7/CD62L-dependent migration, distinguishing them from the more tissue-resident ILC2/ILC3.

title: Characterization of innate lymphoid cells (ILC) in human skin and blood demonstrates increase of NKp44+ ILC3 in psoriasis
quotes: Characterization of innate lymphoid cells (ILC) in human skin and blood demonstrates increase of NKp44+ ILC3 in psoriasis
summary: Villanova et al. (2014) characterise ILCs in human skin, demonstrating their presence in the dermis and the increase of ILC3 in psoriasis, providing context for ILC1/3 plasticity described by Reynolds.
